page: Amazon Web Services r8gb.2xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by an AWS Graviton processor. It features 8 dedicated physical cores on the ARMv9 architecture (Neoverse-V2) running at 2.8 GHz, with 1.0 thread per core. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It operates on the Nitro hypervisor and includes a baseline network bandwidth of 17 Gbps. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance.

Benchmark data reveals strong memory performance, with a PassMark memory score in the top tier (top 10%) and strong large-block memory write bandwidth of 86.39 GB/sec. Single-core performance is also strong, as indicated by a Geekbench single-core score of 1893.0 and strong single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression rates. However, cryptographic performance is a notable bottleneck, with the PassMark CPU encryption test scoring in the weak category. Additionally, while text processing benchmarks are strong, LLM inference tasks show mixed results, with text generation on a 135M model rated as weak and prompt processing on a 7B model rated as poor.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8gb.2xlarge offers high memory density per core, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or high-performance cryptography. The dedicated physical cores provide predictable compute performance for database operations, caching layers, and memory-intensive applications. It is suitable for workloads such as Redis deployments, memory-intensive database operations, and general-purpose memory-optimized tasks.
description: The Amazon Web Services r8gb.2xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized server instance powered by an 8-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core, and runs on the Nitro hypervisor with a 17 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs. Benchmark results highlight top-tier memory performance and strong single-core capabilities, though cryptographic operations and certain LLM inference tasks show weaker relative performance. This instance is designed for memory-intensive workloads such as database operations, caching layers, and memory-bound applications that benefit from dedicated physical cores and high memory density without requiring local NVMe storage.

tagline: An ARM64 memory-optimized instance featuring dedicated physical cores and top-tier memory performance for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.
